question: B-001-identifiers-provenance
claim: Captured records get time-ordered ids (free chronological range-scan); only DERIVED records get content-hash ids (deliberately NOT time-sortable = honest signal); every stored time field supplied explicitly, never read back from an id timestamp. Internal causal edge_id is plain numeric autoincrement, no time-ordered/content-addressed distinction.

question: B-001-identifiers-provenance
claim: Refuse writes deviating > tolerance either direction (bounds replay/storm); wall-clock read used only to accept/reject, NEVER stored (so it never becomes a guessed timestamp); tolerance bounded ~5min, zero-window=config error. Hardens the existing replay path.

question: B-001-identifiers-provenance
claim: Content-hash dedup keys on CONTENT not id, so id-uniqueness needs a SEPARATE explicit collision guard (a write whose subject_id already names a live-or-soft-forgotten record is refused). Internal receipt cache keys on (operation,content_hash,fingerprint) only.

question: B-001-identifiers-provenance
claim: Coarse caller error (unknown writer/bad sig/unsigned/collision all return ONE rejection) so the substrate is neither enrollment- nor forge-oracle; clock-skew reported distinctly so honest clients resync; a backend read-fail surfaces as an availability error WITHOUT a security audit. Generalizable error-hygiene; internal idempotency emits specific cause.

question: B-001-identifiers-provenance
claim: Host holds private key & signs, substrate holds only public key & verifies (no private key in-process); unknown writer refused fail-closed, no self-enrollment. SCOPE-FLAG: likely out-of-scope for a single-trusted-host local MCP; ln lineage is unsigned today.

question: B-001-identifiers-provenance
claim: canonical(subject_id, writer_agent_id, ingested_at) domain-separated + length-prefixed + version-byte so field splits can't collide and a sig under one layout never validates under another. SCOPE-FLAG with writer-signing. Relates to C5 determinism.

question: B-001-identifiers-provenance
claim: Substrate signs its OWN audit events with a dedicated audit key (keygen confined+CI-enforced); consolidation-authored memory stays UNSIGNED (no external writer; signing it = attestation, out-of-scope). Internal derived causal edges have no attestation.

question: B-001-identifiers-provenance
claim: Transport-agnostic hardening (dual-class id PROMOTE + clock-skew + id-collision + oracle-errors, all S) applies cleanly and pairs with C4-A; the writer-signing trio is scope-flagged for a single-trusted-host tool (record, don't promote ahead of hardening). newInfoRatio 0.75.
